title: PDF attachments are corrupted after upload
body: When customers upload PDF files to their tasks, the files come through broken. You can't open them — the PDF viewer says the file is damaged. This is happening for multiple customers. We use the file attachment feature heavily so this is really impacting our workflow.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — corrupted file uploads are definitely something we want to get to the bottom of quickly.

To help us investigate, could you walk us through the exact steps you're taking when uploading a PDF? For example:

1. Where in the app are you uploading (task detail view, bulk upload, drag-and-drop, etc.)?
2. What happens after you upload — does the upload appear to succeed, or do you see any errors during the process?
3. When you try to open the corrupted file, are you downloading it from TaskFlow and opening locally, or using an in-app preview?

This will help us narrow down where in the pipeline the file is getting damaged.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Look, I don't know all the technical details of how the upload works. We just click the attachment button on a task, pick the PDF, it uploads, says it's done with no errors, and then when anyone tries to download and open it, it's garbage. We've tried both the task detail view and drag-and-drop, same result. We download and open locally. It's been like this for about a week and our team is stuck. Can you just fix it?

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thank you for those details — that's really helpful, and I understand the urgency. We're nearly at the point where we can hand this to a developer.

One more thing that will help us reproduce this on our end: **what browser and operating system is your team using?** (For example, Chrome on Windows 11, Safari on macOS, etc.) And if you happen to know your TaskFlow version — it's usually shown at the bottom of the Settings page — that would be great too.

Also, roughly how large are the PDFs that are getting corrupted? And does it happen with all PDFs, or only certain ones (e.g., larger files, or files with specific content like images or forms)?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Chrome on Windows 10, but it's happening for everyone on our team regardless of browser so I don't think that's it. I don't know the version off the top of my head, sorry. As for file size — now that you mention it, I think the smaller PDFs might actually be fine? Like the little one-pagers seem to come through okay. It's the bigger reports and contracts that are messed up. But honestly I'm not 100% sure on that. Can someone just look into this? It's been a week already.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"PDF attachments corrupted after upload — likely file-size-dependent","problem":"PDF files uploaded to tasks via the attachment button or drag-and-drop are corrupted when downloaded. The PDF viewer reports the file as damaged. This affects multiple customers and has been occurring for approximately one week. No errors are shown during the upload process itself.","root_cause_hypothesis":"A recent change (approximately one week ago) likely introduced a bug in the file upload or storage pipeline that truncates or corrupts larger files. Possible causes include: a misconfigured multipart upload threshold or chunk size, a timeout on the upload endpoint that silently truncates large request bodies, or an encoding issue (e.g., base64 vs binary) that only manifests when file size exceeds a buffer boundary.","reproduction_steps":["Open a task in the task detail view","Click the attachment button (or drag-and-drop a file)","Select a multi-page PDF (e.g., >1 MB — a report or contract)","Wait for the upload to complete (should show success, no errors)","Download the uploaded file","Attempt to open the downloaded PDF — it should be reported as damaged/corrupted","Repeat with a small single-page PDF for comparison — this may succeed"],"environment":"Chrome on Windows 10 (but reported across multiple browsers and team members). TaskFlow version unknown.","severity":"high","impact":"Multiple customers are unable to use the file attachment feature for larger PDF documents. Teams that rely heavily on attachments are blocked. The issue has persisted for approximately one week.","recommended_fix":"1. Check deployment history for changes ~1 week ago to the file upload pipeline, storage layer, or any proxy/gateway configuration (e.g., request body size limits, timeout changes). 2. Upload a large PDF and compare the byte size and checksum of the original file vs. the stored file to determine where corruption occurs (upload, storage, or download). 3. Inspect whether multipart upload chunking or encoding (binary vs. base64) was recently changed. 4. Check for any reverse proxy or CDN configuration changes that may truncate large responses.","proposed_test_case":"Upload PDFs of varying sizes (e.g., 100 KB, 1 MB, 5 MB, 20 MB) via both the attachment button and drag-and-drop. Download each and verify the file checksum matches the original. Assert that all downloaded files open without corruption in a PDF reader.","information_gaps":["Exact TaskFlow version in use","Precise file size threshold at which corruption begins (reporter was uncertain)","Whether non-PDF file types are also affected","Whether the stored file on the server is already corrupted or only the downloaded copy"]}
